chapter: Chapter 8: Measurement of Time and Motion

questions.question: Define speed.
questions.answer: Speed is the distance travelled by an object per unit time.

questions.question: Write the formula for speed.
questions.answer: Speed = Distance / Time.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of speed?
questions.answer: The SI unit of speed is metre per second (m/s).

questions.question: What does a speedometer measure?
questions.answer: A speedometer measures the instantaneous speed of a vehicle.

questions.question: What does an odometer measure?
questions.answer: An odometer measures the total distance travelled by a vehicle.

questions.question: What is motion?
questions.answer: An object is said to be in motion if its position changes with time.

questions.question: What is uniform motion?
questions.answer: Uniform motion occurs when an object travels equal distances in equal intervals of time.

questions.question: What is non-uniform motion?
questions.answer: Non-uniform motion occurs when an object travels unequal distances in equal intervals of time.

questions.question: Name the instrument used to measure time.
questions.answer: A clock or stopwatch is used to measure time.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of time?
questions.answer: The SI unit of time is second (s).

questions.question: Calculate the speed of a car that travels 120 km in 2 hours.
questions.answer: Speed = 120 / 2 = 60 km/h.

questions.question: Convert 36 km/h into m/s.
What does a straight line in a distance–time graph represent?
questions.answer: It represents uniform motion.

questions.question: What does a curved line in a distance–time graph represent?
questions.answer: It represents non-uniform motion.

questions.question: How many seconds are there in one minute?
questions.answer: There are 60 seconds in one minute.

questions.question: How many minutes are there in one hour?
questions.answer: There are 60 minutes in one hour.

questions.question: Name a natural unit of time measurement.
questions.answer: Day is a natural unit of time based on Earth's rotation.

questions.question: What is average speed?
questions.answer: Average speed is the total distance travelled divided by total time taken.

questions.question: If a cyclist covers 30 km in 1.5 hours, what is the speed?
questions.answer: Speed = 30 / 1.5 = 20 km/h.

questions.question: What type of motion does a pendulum show?
questions.answer: A pendulum shows periodic motion.

questions.question: Define periodic motion.
questions.answer: Periodic motion is motion that repeats itself at equal intervals of time.

questions.question: What is the distance travelled if a vehicle moves at 40 km/h for 2 hours?
questions.answer: Distance = Speed × Time = 40 × 2 = 80 km.

questions.question: Which graph is used to represent motion visually?
questions.answer: A distance–time graph is used to represent motion visually.

questions.question: If distance increases steadily with time, what type of motion is it?
questions.answer: It is uniform motion.

questions.question: What happens to speed if distance increases but time remains constant?
questions.answer: Speed increases.
